skill.name: github-pr
skill.description: GitHub pull request helpers for triage, guideline discovery, and review comments using Bun and the GitHub CLI.
skill.summary: Use authenticated GitHub CLI helpers to decide when to review PRs, find project guidance, and post review comments.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-crypto patterns, but the cited high crypto findings are false positives in markdown metadata and overview text. The real risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to run authenticated Bun and GitHub CLI commands that can read repository guidance files and publish PR comments.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated GitHub CLI Commands Can Modify PR Discussions
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Bun helpers and direct gh commands that post inline or regular PR comments. This is legitimate for a PR review skill, but it can publish content under the authenticated GitHub account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands clearly post comments to GitHub. The intent is legitimate, but the action mutates external project state.

medium_findings.title: Repository Guidance Files May Be Read and Emitted
medium_findings.description: The list-guideline-files command finds AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md files and can output their contents as JSON. This appears scoped to repository guidance, but it can expose local project instructions in logs or chat transcripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes reading and outputting file contents. No evidence shows secret harvesting or external exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Is Required for Core Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Bun and GitHub CLI execution for PR checks, guideline lookup, diffs, API calls, and comments. This is expected for the domain, but users should run it only in trusted repositories and authenticated contexts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command usage is directly documented and central to the skill. I found no shell injection or hidden command construction in the available files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at the description and overview lines. These lines contain plain markdown metadata and prose, with no hash function or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible text only. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, or any cryptographic API exists at those locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Bun and GitHub CLI documentation in the prerequisites section. They are not network calls and do not transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are markdown links for installation prerequisites. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, or credential-bearing request at these lines.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are code spans or fenced command examples in SKILL.md. They describe shell usage for a GitHub helper skill, but the markdown file itself does not execute Ruby backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations are markdown code spans and command examples, not Ruby source. The commands still create operational risk when an agent chooses to run them.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated External Command Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on local execution of Bun scripts and gh commands authenticated to GitHub. Reviewers should confirm each command before allowing actions that post comments or call GitHub APIs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow depends on authenticated external commands. I did not find hidden persistence, obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious endpoints.

content.user_title: Review GitHub Pull Requests with CLI Helpers
content.value_statement: Manual PR triage can waste review time and miss project-specific guidance. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code check PR readiness, find guidance files, and draft review comments through GitHub CLI workflows.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a pull request should be reviewed before spending review time.
Skips closed, merged, draft, bot-created, previously reviewed, or trivial PRs.
Finds AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md files relevant to modified PR paths.
Can output guideline file paths and contents for review context.
Posts inline PR comments, including multi-line comments and suggestion blocks.
Shows related gh CLI commands for viewing PR metadata, diffs, comments, and files.
limitations: Requires Bun and an authenticated GitHub CLI installation.
Does not include the referenced source scripts in this package snapshot.
Operates only where the local GitHub account has repository access.
Does not replace human judgment for final code review decisions.

use_cases.title: Triage PR Review Queues
use_cases.description: Decide whether a PR deserves review based on draft status, bot authorship, previous AI review, and change size.

use_cases.title: Collect Repository Review Guidance
use_cases.description: Find the AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md files that apply to files changed in a pull request.

use_cases.title: Post Focused Review Feedback
use_cases.description: Create inline comments or suggestion blocks on exact PR lines through authenticated GitHub CLI helpers.

prompt_templates.title: Check Whether to Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage before opening a full review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub PR skill to check whether the current branch PR should be reviewed. Explain the reason before reviewing code.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Gather project instructions before a review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub PR skill to list guidance files for PR 123. Summarize the rules that apply to changed files.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Inline Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn review findings into precise comments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review PR 123 and prepare inline comments for confirmed issues. Include file paths, line numbers, and concise markdown bodies.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Guided Review Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced end-to-end PR review with safeguards.
prompt_templates.prompt: For PR 123, check review eligibility, gather relevant guidance, inspect the diff, and propose inline comments. Ask before posting anything to GitHub.

output_examples.input: Check whether the current PR needs review.
output_examples.output: The PR should be reviewed because it is open, not a draft, not bot-authored, and has meaningful code changes.

output: Root project guidance applies to all changed files.
A component-specific guidance file applies to the modified frontend directory.

output_examples.input: Prepare an inline comment for a missing error path.
output_examples.output: Suggested comment: This branch should handle the failed authentication path before returning success to the caller.

best_practices: Confirm the active GitHub account and repository before running commands.
Review generated comments before posting them to a pull request.
Read relevant AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md guidance before judging code changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run authenticated PR commands in untrusted repositories without review.
Do not post AI-generated comments without checking file paths and line numbers.
Do not treat the review-needed check as a final quality decision.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the GitHub CLI and requires an authenticated gh session.

faq.question: Can it post comments to pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents helpers for inline comments, multi-line comments, and suggestion blocks.

faq.question: Does it decide whether every PR is safe?
faq.answer: No. It only checks review eligibility signals. Humans still decide review quality and merge readiness.

faq.question: Can it read project instruction files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can find AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md files related to changed PR paths.

faq.question: Is this limited to Claude?
faq.answer: No. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I check before posting feedback?
faq.answer: Verify the repository, PR number, file path, line number, and comment text before posting.
